architecture_notes: Facade plus backend adapters: the parent keeps the uniform trait, picks a peer's winning URL across primary and fallbacks, applies the dry-run reachability rule, and delegates each connect and filesystem operation to the per-scheme backend that owns it.

LocalBackend and SftpBackend implement the same operation set for their scheme, so the shared operation line-items (022.2 through 022.18) are assigned to both; the identical-results invariants 022.1 and 022.19 stay a parent integration concern.
UrlNormalize is a pure, transport-free string and path transform with no dependency on the backends.

children.name: UrlNormalize
children.responsibility: Turn a peer URL into its deterministic canonical identity: lowercase scheme and host, drop default SFTP port 22, collapse and trim slashes, convert a bare path to an absolute file:// URL, percent-decode unreserved characters, strip the query string, and insert the current OS user for an SFTP URL that omits one.

children.name: LocalBackend
children.responsibility: Implement the uniform filesystem operation set over a local file:// peer, create its root and parents in a normal run while honoring the dry-run no-create rule, and map native errors into the not-found, permission-denied, and I/O-error categories.

children.name: SftpBackend
children.responsibility: Connect an sftp:// peer by trying credential sources in the fixed order, verifying the host key against known_hosts, and bounding the handshake by the connection timeout, then implement the same filesystem operation set over that connection with the same error categories and dry-run-aware root handling.
